channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Treasury Department authorizes Iranian oil sales through August
item.description: Iranian supertankers have switched on their transponders, after going dark during the war, as they depart the region loaded with oil.

item.title: Alan Greenspan, former chairman of the Fed, dies at age 100
item.description: Alan Greenspan presided over the Federal Reserve for 19 years under four presidents and mastered the art of obfuscation known as Fedspeak.

item.title: UK PM Starmer resigns as Britain faces its seventh leader in 10 years
item.description: Keir Starmer said he will resign as U.K. prime minister and Labour leader, triggering a contest to choose his successor.

item.title: S&P 500 is little changed to start the week as oil prices slide on Iran deal progress, Micron leads chips higher: Live updates
item.description: The S&P 500 was relatively unchanged on Monday, while oil prices fell, as Wall Street assessed the latest developments in the Iran war negotiations.

item.title: 'Reward for failure': Investor support for Target chair Brian Cornell falls to lowest level ever
item.description: Target chair Brian Cornell built the retailer into a $100 billion-plus giant, but recent struggles and underperformance have fueled investor calls for change.

item.title: A new analyst is out with coverage on SpaceX. Why KeyBanc thinks the stock could be cut in half
item.description: The investment bank began coverage of the aerospace and satellite company with a Sector Weight rating, equivalent to no more than a hold.

item.title: Lucid to lay off roughly 18% of U.S. workforce, COO Marc Winterhoff leaves
item.description: Lucid Group is cutting its U.S. workforce by 18% as part of a cost-savings plan. It also said COO Marc Winterhoff is departing effective immediately.

item.title: UPS to invest $48 million in temperature-controlled facilities amid healthcare boom
item.description: UPS is announcing a new investment in healthcare logistics as demand for the temperature-controlled category booms, CNBC has learned exclusively.

item.title: Chevron to fuel massive Microsoft data center in Texas using natural gas
item.description: Microsoft's embrace of natural gas shows a willingness to invest in fossil fuels to meet the power demand needed in its data centers.

item.title: SpaceX makes bond sale days after record IPO, discloses over $100 billion cash pile
item.description: SpaceX on Monday unveiled a senior unsecured notes offering and said it held about $100.8 billion in cash.

item.title: Companies are demanding states cut red tape. Data center-wary voters may think differently
item.description: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro touted a $20 billion economic development deal last June, the state's largest ever. It's caused Shapiro no shortage of grief.

item.title: The No. 1 question ultra-high net worth families should ask a financial advisor
item.description: The financial needs of the uber-rich are often complex. Here's how an ultra-high net worth family can gauge a prospective financial advisor.

item.title: CNBC Elite Advisors: Top ultra-high net worth wealth management firms for 2026
item.description: CNBC Elite Advisors recognizes wealth management firms serving high net worth and UHNW clients.

item.title: Dozens injured, 18 missing after blast during restart at giant Qatar LNG site
item.description: Authorities said a 'technical accident' occurred at the Barzan local gas supply facility on Sunday evening and that there was no threat to public safety.

item.title: Watch out: Short-term cracks are starting to develop in the stock market
item.description: Investors may want to tread lightly as certain cracks are starting to emerge.

item.title: Building materials group CRH to buy Arcosa in $8.5 billion all-cash deal
item.description: CRH is offering $150 per share ⁠for ‌Arcosa, representing a 10.4% premium to Arcosa's previous ⁠close.

item.title: Raising the minimum wage has been a big political winner. Now it's running into resistance
item.description: Raising the minimum wage has been a progressive policy winner at the ballot box, but recent losses suggest an economic mood slowing its political momentum.

item.title: SpaceX stock falls 8%, pacing for third-straight day of losses after red-hot IPO start
item.description: A rally following the company's record breaking IPO on June 12 has cooled, with stock dropping the past two full days of trading.

item.title: AbbVie bets $10.9 billion on Apogee in next-generation immunology growth push
item.description: AbbVie said on Monday it would buy biotech Apogee ⁠Therapeutics for $10.9 billion, its largest buyout in more than five years.

item.title: China imposes trade curbs on dozens of U.S. firms in retaliation for Pentagon blacklist
item.description: The Pentagon earlier this month updated its so-called 1260H list by adding a slew of Chinese technology companies to a roster of entities it believes have aided Beijing's military.

item.title: The Club's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Monday
item.description: Abbvie makes a move in M&A, and SpaceX is started with a hold rating at KeyBanc.

item.title: Prime Day 2026: 6 tools and products that actually save money
item.description: Amazon Prime Day is June 23 to June 26. Here are six guidelines, tools and products to make sure you're getting the best deal.

item.title: The new arms race in wealth management: Winning the ultra-wealthy
item.description: Most registered investment advisors say they serve wealthy clients. The CNBC Elite Advisors list aims to help ultra-high net worth investors narrow the field.

item.title: Why a 66-year-old water treaty is becoming the latest India-Pakistan flashpoint
item.description: India and Pakistan are locked in a dispute over the Indus Water Treaty after New Delhi suspended the pact, raising concerns about regional stability.

item.title: Oil prices fall after Vance says U.S. and Iran made progress in peace talks
item.description: The discussions at the Swiss resort of Bürgenstock marked the first negotiations since Washington and Tehran signed a memorandum of understanding last week.

item.title: Pixar's 'Toy Story 5' lassos biggest opening weekend in franchise history with $160 million haul
item.description: Disney and Pixar's "Toy Story 5" tallied $160 million domestically during its opening weekend, the highest debut in franchise history.

item.title: 2-year Treasury note yield hits highest since February 2025
item.description: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — rose four basis points, to 4.491%.

item.title: Tencent tests AI assistant in China's most popular app as it looks to catch up with rivals
item.description: WeChat is an indispensable part of daily life in China and Tencent is trying to tap into its huge user base to expand use of its AI services.

item.title: SNAP restrictions could change what shoppers buy — and food giants are watching
item.description: SNAP food restrictions are spreading to more states, pressuring major food and beverage as consumers shift spending away from soda, candy and processed foods.

item.title: Defense giant KNDS moves closer to IPO after France-Germany stake deal
item.description: France and Germany agreed on a KNDS framework as Germany seeks a 40% stake ahead of a potential multibillion-euro IPO.
